mission.title: Install and Configure Tetragon on Kubernetes
mission.description: Tetragon is an eBPF-based security observability and runtime enforcement tool from the Cilium project. It detects and can react to security-significant events such as process execution, system call activity, and network or file I/O, with Kubernetes-aware context. Installing Tetragon gives platform teams deep visibility into workload behavior at the kernel level.

steps.title: Add the Cilium Helm repository
steps.description:
Tetragon is distributed through the Cilium Helm repository. Add it to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add cilium https://helm.cilium.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Tetragon using Helm
steps.description:
Install Tetragon into the 'kube-system' namespace, which is the recommended location per the upstream docs.
```bash
helm install tetragon cilium/tetragon --version 1.6.1 --namespace kube-system
```

steps.title: Wait for the DaemonSet to roll out
steps.description:
Tetragon deploys as a DaemonSet so every node gets an agent pod. Watch the rollout until all pods are ready.
```bash
kubectl rollout status -n kube-system ds/tetragon -w
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Confirm the Tetragon pods are running and the CRDs are registered.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon
kubectl get crd | grep cilium.io
```

steps.title: Observe process execution events
steps.description:
Tail the export log on one of the agent pods to confirm events are being generated.
```bash
kubectl exec -n kube-system ds/tetragon -c export-stdout -- tail -f /var/run/cilium/tetragon/tetragon.log
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Tetragon Helm release from the 'kube-system' namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall tetragon --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up tracing policies and CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any TracingPolicy resources and the Tetragon CRDs that were installed.
```bash
kubectl delete tracingpolicies --all
kubectl delete crd tracingpolicies.cilium.io tracingpoliciesnamespaced.cilium.io podinfo.cilium.io
```

uninstall.title: Verify removal
uninstall.description:
Confirm no Tetragon pods or DaemonSets remain in 'kube-system'.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon
kubectl get ds -n kube-system tetragon
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current Helm values and any tracing policies before upgrading.
```bash
helm get values tetragon --namespace kube-system > tetragon-backup.yaml
kubectl get tracingpolicies -o yaml > tracingpolicies-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Refresh the Cilium Helm repository to pick up the latest Tetragon chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Tetragon
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the release to the next minor version. The chart version mirrors the application version for Tetragon.
```bash
helm upgrade tetragon cilium/tetragon --namespace kube-system --version 1.7.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the DaemonSet rolled out and the new agent pods are running the expected image.
```bash
kubectl rollout status -n kube-system ds/tetragon -w
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].image}'
```

troubleshooting.title: Tetragon pods stuck in Init or CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Tetragon needs a recent kernel with BTF support. Check the agent logs for a kernel or BTF error before anything else.
```bash
kubectl logs -n kube-system ds/tetragon -c tetragon
```
On kind clusters, make sure the host /proc is mounted into the node container as described in the Tetragon install-k8s guide.

troubleshooting.title: No events appearing in the export log
troubleshooting.description:
If the export-stdout container shows nothing, confirm the Tetragon process is healthy and that events are not being filtered out by the default kube-system exclusion.
```bash
kubectl logs -n kube-system ds/tetragon -c tetragon
kubectl logs -n kube-system ds/tetragon -c export-stdout
```

troubleshooting.title: TracingPolicy not taking effect
troubleshooting.description:
If a custom TracingPolicy is not producing events, verify it was accepted and is loaded on the agents.
```bash
kubectl get tracingpolicies
kubectl exec -n kube-system ds/tetragon -c tetragon -- tetra tracingpolicy list
```

troubleshooting.title: High CPU or memory usage on an agent pod
troubleshooting.description:
Noisy tracing policies can load the agent. Check which policies are active and consider scoping them to specific namespaces.
```bash
kubectl top pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon
kubectl get tracingpolicies
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Tetragon will show one agent pod per node in 'kube-system', the Tetragon CRDs registered on the cluster, and process execution events streaming through the export-stdout container. From here you can apply TracingPolicy resources to enforce custom runtime rules.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cilium https://helm.cilium.io
helm repo update
kubectl exec -n kube-system ds/tetragon -c export-stdout -- tail -f /var/run/cilium/tetragon/tetragon.log

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running on Linux nodes with a kernel that supports eBPF and BTF, and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine. On kind clusters the host /proc filesystem must be mounted into the node container.
